claims.currentEvidenceStatus: proof-planning; not satisfied by this catalog entry alone.
claims.nextAction: keep discovery source-scope tests and status summaries connected to U1/U8, then review remaining false-positive and false-negative boundaries through catalog-level decisions.
claims.absorbs: raw claims about source inventory, entry-doc discovery, `.gitignore`, duplicate handling, stale state, and proof-debt visibility.

claims.title: The Binary And Skill Own Different Parts Of The Claim Workflow
claims.currentEvidenceStatus: proof-planning; some command and skill checks exist, but this catalog entry is not satisfied until the matching evidence refs are attached in the claim packet.
claims.nextAction: keep binary help/disclosure checks deterministic and prove claim-review routing through the checked dev/skill fixture surface.
claims.absorbs: raw claims about command discovery, packet examples, deterministic scans, validation, skill routing, review budgets, and LLM-backed claim review.

claims.title: Host Repos Own Runtime-Specific Behavior
claims.nextAction: prefer deterministic adapter and live-run invocation checks over human review for packet ownership, while leaving host-owned runner decisions explicit.
claims.absorbs: raw claims about adapters, prompts, wrappers, fixtures, credentials, runtime launch, backend/model choice, repo-specific flags, and product-owned schemas.

claims.title: Evaluation Uses Two Surfaces And Four Presets
claims.nextAction: keep normalizer, fixture, and public-spec proof aligned with `dev/repo`, `dev/skill`, `app/chat`, and `app/prompt`.
claims.absorbs: raw claims about the `dev`/`app` split, preset vocabulary, fixture-declared surfaces, and retirement of older archetype wording.

claims.title: Review And Evidence Are Separate State Transitions
claims.nextAction: keep review-result application and validation tests strict enough that possible evidence or human agreement cannot satisfy a claim.
claims.absorbs: raw claims about review labels, proof route updates, evidence refs, possible evidence, and satisfaction semantics.

claims.title: Review-Result Replay Must Guard Semantic Drift
claims.currentEvidenceStatus: proof-planning; fingerprint-guard implementation exists and should remain covered by replay-helper tests.
claims.nextAction: keep review-result replay tests focused on missing IDs and mismatched fingerprints whenever claim discovery IDs or line positions change.
claims.absorbs: raw claims about historical review-result replay, stale IDs, line-based ID reuse, fingerprints, and audit history.

claims.title: Runner Readiness Is Based On Declared Runtime Inputs
claims.nextAction: keep `doctor` and `agent status` on shared runner-readiness semantics, and prove adapter hash, runner file hash, and harmless commit drift cases.
claims.absorbs: raw claims about runner assessment, adapter hash drift, runner file drift, commit drift provenance, and readiness branch behavior.

claims.title: Human Views Mirror Packets
claims.proofRoute: deterministic plus browser/runtime checks where useful.
claims.nextAction: keep Markdown/HTML/status-server views packet-backed and use browser/runtime proof when the reader surface itself changes.
claims.absorbs: raw claims about JSON source-of-truth, Markdown reports, HTML reports, status server state, comments, and stale report avoidance.

claims.title: Optimization Requires An Honest Proof Surface
claims.nextAction: prove optimization packet boundaries before promoting any optimizer output as a recommended behavior change.
claims.absorbs: raw claims about optimize, GEPA-style search, budgets, checkpoints, protected validation, frontier promotion, and revision artifacts.

claims.title: Public Specs And Claim Catalogs Have Different Jobs
claims.currentEvidenceStatus: proof-planning; README and link checks can prove reachability, while maintainer review must approve the catalog content.
claims.nextAction: review U1-U8 first, then M1-M10, before returning to raw action-bucket cards.
claims.absorbs: raw claims about claim catalogs, public specs, raw discovery output, reader-facing promise maps, and report/source boundaries.
